zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Bermingham於1288年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾比米加姆時，他的父親William de Bermingham26歲，母親Matilda Deincourt20歲。他於大約1312年與Mathilde de Geneville在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1335年在加拿大亞伯達省卡姆洛斯 庫迪 諾 2芭特爾享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭韋斯特 米德藍茲比米加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Bermingham于1288年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾比米加姆时，他的父亲William de Bermingham26岁，母亲Matilda Deincourt20岁。他于大约1312年与Mathilde de Geneville在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾沃里克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1335年在加拿大亚伯达省卡姆洛斯 庫迪 諾 2芭特爾享年47岁，埋葬在英国英格兰韋斯特 米德藍茲比米加姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bermingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Sinead.
Irish (of English origin): variant of   Birmingham  . This form of the surname is found mainly in Ireland.

History: The Bermingham family take their surname from lands held at what is now a great city in the West Midlands of England, but it was brought to Ireland as early as the 12th century. The Norman baron Robert de Bermyngeham was a follower of Strongbow (see Clare ); his name is found on a number of Irish charters between 1175 and 1179. The family was also known as Mac Fheorais (see Corish ) in Ireland, named for Piers (Gaelic Feoras) de Bermyngeham, the father of Robert.

variants.name: Birmingham
meanings: English: habitational name from Birmingham in the West Midlands. In Domesday Book the name is already found as Bermingeham, but it seems likely that it was originally Beornmundingahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the people of (-inga-) Beornmund’, a personal name composed of the elements beorn ‘young man, warrior’ + mund ‘protection’. This surname is established in Ireland (see Bermingham ).

variants.name: Corish
meanings: shortened form of Mac Fheorais ‘son of Piers’. The name was borne by a Connacht family descended from Piers de Bermingham, second lord of Athenry in Galway (died c. 1309). Compare Bermingham .
shortened form of Mac Muirghis ‘son of Maurice’, a personal name introduced to Ireland by the Normans. Compare Morrissey .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Bermingham was born in 1288, in Birmingham, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de Bermingham, was 26 and his mother, Matilda Deincourt, was 20. He married Mathilde de Geneville about 1312, in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1335, in Battle, Camrose County No. 22, Alberta, Canada, at the age of 47, and was buried in Birmingham, West Midlands, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Bermingham est né en 1288, à Birmingham, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Bermingham, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Matilda Deincourt, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde de Geneville environ 1312, à Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1335, à Battle, Camrose County No. 22, Alberta, Canada, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Birmingham, West Midlands, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat um 1312 in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom Mathilde de Geneville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1335 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Battle, Camrose County No. 22, Alberta, Kanada und wurde in Birmingham, West Midlands, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Berminghamが1288年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアバーミンガムで生まれた時，父William de Berminghamは26歳，母Matilda Deincourtは20歳であった。約1312年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックでMathilde de Genevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1335年にカナダアルバータ州カムローゼ コウントイ ノ. 2バットルで47歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウエスト ミドランズバーミンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.Battle of Evesham: 캐나다 앨버타 캄로즈 카운티 노. 2 바틀
ko.lifeSummary: William de Bermingham(이)가 1288년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 비밍함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Bermingham(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Deincourt(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅에서 Mathilde de Geneville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1335년 캐나다 앨버타 캄로즈 카운티 노. 2 바틀에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 미들랜드즈 비밍함에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Mathilde de Geneville aproximadamente 1312, em Warwick, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. ru.Battle of Evesham: баттл, камрос коунти но. 2, Альберта, Канада
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Bermingham родился в 1288 г. в местности бирмингхам, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de Bermingham было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Deincourt, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde de Geneville около 1312 г. в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1335 г. в местности баттл, камрос коунти но. 2, Альберта, Канада в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности бирмингхам, вест мидландс, Англия, Великобритания.
